ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON NOV-24-2009 IN DEPT. 206, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PREFERENCE IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(A). THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 3/22/10 @ 11:15AM IN DEPT. 206. PLAINTIFF TO CONTACT DEPT. 502 FOR MSC DATES. DISCOVERY CUT OFF ON 3/19/10. ANY AND ALL DISCOVERY MOTIONS WILL BE HEARD IN DEPT. 206. MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT CAN BE HEARD UP TO TRIAL ON 45 DAYS NOTICE PURSUANT TO STIPULATION. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL IDENTIFICATION AND LAY WITNESSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS THAT SUPPORT THEIR TESTIMONY, AS TO EACH INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT BY 12/28/09. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES AFTER THE DUE DATE THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT IDENTIFIED PREVIOUSLY. ALL DEFENDANTS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL LAY WITNESSES THAT THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT LEARNED OF THROUGH ITS OWN DISCOVERY EFFORTS AND THAT WILL SUPPORT PROPOSITION 51 PURPOSES OR FOR THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS UPON WHICH THESE WITNESSES SUPPORT, AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 1/19/10. IF DEFENDANTS LEARN OF NEW WITNESSES AND OR DOCUMENTS, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED PREVIOUSLY. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. ANY PARTY SEEKING DISCOVERY ENFORCEMENT OR SANCTION MAY APPLY FOR OST, IF GRANTED, MOTIONS HEARD ON 5 DAYS NOTICE. OPPOSITIONS DUE 2 DAYS AND REPLIES WILL BE WAIVED. STATUS CONFERENCES ARE SCHEDULED FOR 12/4/09, 12/11/09 AND 12/18/09 AT 3:30PM IN D.206. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER AND APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE: JAMES J. ASBESTOS MOTION IN DEPT. 206, DEFENDANT RPM, INC.S JOINDER IN DEFENDANT CERTAINTEED CORPORATIONS MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS COMPLIANCE REGARDING WORSITE PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND PERCIPIENT WITNESS DISCLOSURE. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED FROM JAN-21-2010 TO ASBESTOS MOTION AT FEB-04-2010, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. BASED ON THE EVIDENCE AND ARGUMENT, THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS ENGAGED IN WILLFUL DISOBEDIENCE OF THE COURT'S DISCOVERY ORDER BY ENGAGING IN A PATTERN OF DELIBERATE OVER DISCLOSURE SOMETIMES REFERRED TO AS BOXED CAR DISCOVERY. THE DISCLOSURE SHALL BE NARROWLY TAILORED & WILL CONTAIN COMPLETE INFORMATION WHICH WILL BE SUPPLEMENTED WITH A DECLARATION UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY FROM A LAWYER KNOWLEDGEABLE OF THE INVESTIGATION ON OR BEFORE 1/26/10. THE DISCOVERY ORDER PREVIOUSLY WILL BE MODIFIED THAT IT WILL REMAIN OPEN UNTIL ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL DEPT. THE DISCOVERY IS FURTHER MODIFIED & AMENDED, ALTHOUGH THE COURT DOES NOT RECALL OR BELIEVE THAT PLAINTIFF RAISED ANY OBJECTION TO THIS DISCOVERY ORDER ON THE BASIS OF THE WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE PURSUANT TO SNYDER V. CATERPILLAR, INC. 157 CAL.APP.4TH 1530, THE COURT MAKES A FINDING THAT THE RECIPROCAL DISCOVERY ORDER IS AIMED AT ACCOMPLISHING & SUSTAINING THE PARTIES RIGHTS TO DUE PROCESS IN ORDER TO PREPARE FOR A TRIAL THAT WAS SET ON 110 DAYS NOTICE. THE COURT OVERCOMES ANY ASSERTION OF THE WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE WITH RESPECT TO THE DISCLOSURE OF THE NAMES, ADDRESSES & PHONE NUMBERS OF WITNESSES & EVIDENCE BECAUSE A PARTY AND ANY DEFENDANT WOULD BE UNFAIRLY PREJUDICED IF NORMAL DISCOVERY RULES HAD TO BE FOLLOWED IN ORDER TO GET THIS CASE PREPARED FOR TRIAL. THE COURT SPECIFICALLY RESERVES THE ISSUE OF WHETHER OR NOT TRIAL NEEDS TO BE VACATED TO ACCOMMODATE THE DUE PROCESS RIGHTS OF THE MOVING PARTIES. SANCTION HEARING SET FOR 2/4/10. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: R.VELUZ (PLTF'S CSR: RITA LERNER #3179)

BASED ON THE EVIDENCE AND ARGUMENT, THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS ENGAGED IN WILLFUL DISOBEDIENCE OF THE COURT'S DISCOVERY ORDER BY ENGAGING IN A PATTERN OF DELIBERATE OVER DISCLOSURE SOMETIMES REFERRED TO AS BOXED CAR DISCOVERY. THE DISCLOSURE SHALL BE NARROWLY TAILORED & WILL CONTAIN COMPLETE INFORMATION WHICH WILL BE SUPPLEMENTED WITH A DECLARATION UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY FROM A LAWYER KNOWLEDGEABLE OF THE INVESTIGATION ON OR BEFORE 1/26/10. THE DISCOVERY ORDER PREVIOUSLY WILL BE MODIFIED THAT IT WILL REMAIN OPEN UNTIL ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL DEPT. THE DISCOVERY IS FURTHER MODIFIED & AMENDED, ALTHOUGH THE COURT DOES NOT RECALL OR BELIEVE THAT PLAINTIFF RAISED ANY OBJECTION TO THIS DISCOVERY ORDER ON THE BASIS OF THE WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE PURSUANT TO SNYDER V. CATERPILLAR, INC. 157 CAL.APP.4TH 1530, THE COURT MAKES A FINDING THAT THE RECIPROCAL DISCOVERY ORDER IS AIMED AT ACCOMPLISHING & SUSTAINING THE PARTIES RIGHTS TO DUE PROCESS IN ORDER TO PREPARE FOR A TRIAL THAT WAS SET ON 110 DAYS NOTICE. THE COURT OVERCOMES ANY ASSERTION OF THE WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE WITH RESPECT TO THE DISCLOSURE OF THE NAMES, ADDRESSES & PHONE NUMBERS OF WITNESSES & EVIDENCE BECAUSE A PARTY AND ANY DEFENDANT WOULD BE UNFAIRLY PREJUDICED IF NORMAL DISCOVERY RULES HAD TO BE FOLLOWED IN ORDER TO GET THIS CASE PREPARED FOR TRIAL. THE COURT SPECIFICALLY RESERVES THE ISSUE OF WHETHER OR NOT TRIAL NEEDS TO BE VACATED TO ACCOMMODATE THE DUE PROCESS RIGHTS OF THE MOVING PARTIES. SANCTION HEARING SET FOR 2/4/10. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: R.VELUZ (PLTF'S CSR: RITA LERNER #3179)

ASBESTOS MOTION IN DEPT. 206, DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE INC'S JOINDER TO DEFENDANT CERTAINTEED CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCLOSURE OF WITNESSES. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED FROM JAN-21-2010 TO ASBESTOS MOTION AT FEB-04-2010, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. BASED ON THE EVIDENCE AND ARGUMENT, THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS ENGAGED IN WILLFUL DISOBEDIENCE OF THE COURT'S DISCOVERY ORDER BY ENGAGING IN A PATTERN OF DELIBERATE OVER DISCLOSURE SOMETIMES REFERRED TO AS BOXED CAR DISCOVERY. THE DISCLOSURE SHALL BE NARROWLY TAILORED & WILL CONTAIN COMPLETE INFORMATION WHICH WILL BE SUPPLEMENTED WITH A DECLARATION UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY FROM A LAWYER KNOWLEDGEABLE OF THE INVESTIGATION ON OR BEFORE 1/26/10. THE DISCOVERY ORDER PREVIOUSLY WILL BE MODIFIED THAT IT WILL REMAIN OPEN UNTIL ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL DEPT. THE DISCOVERY IS FURTHER MODIFIED & AMENDED, ALTHOUGH THE COURT DOES NOT RECALL OR BELIEVE THAT PLAINTIFF RAISED ANY OBJECTION TO THIS DISCOVERY ORDER ON THE BASIS OF THE WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE PURSUANT TO SNYDER V. CATERPILLAR, INC. 157 CAL.APP.4TH 1530, THE COURT MAKES A FINDING THAT THE RECIPROCAL DISCOVERY ORDER IS AIMED AT ACCOMPLISHING & SUSTAINING THE PARTIES RIGHTS TO DUE PROCESS IN ORDER TO PREPARE FOR A TRIAL THAT WAS SET ON 110 DAYS NOTICE. THE COURT OVERCOMES ANY ASSERTION OF THE WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE WITH RESPECT TO THE DISCLOSURE OF THE NAMES, ADDRESSES & PHONE NUMBERS OF WITNESSES & EVIDENCE BECAUSE A PARTY AND ANY DEFENDANT WOULD BE UNFAIRLY PREJUDICED IF NORMAL DISCOVERY RULES HAD TO BE FOLLOWED IN ORDER TO GET THIS CASE PREPARED FOR TRIAL. THE COURT SPECIFICALLY RESERVES THE ISSUE OF WHETHER OR NOT TRIAL NEEDS TO BE VACATED TO ACCOMMODATE THE DUE PROCESS RIGHTS OF THE MOVING PARTIES. SANCTION HEARING SET FOR 2/4/10. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: R.VELUZ (PLTF'S CSR: RITA LERNER #3179)

ASBESTOS MOTION IN DEPT. 206, DEFENDANT SHELL OIL COMPANY'S JOINDER TO DEFENDANT CERTAINTEED CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S COMPLIANCE RE WORKSITE, PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND PERCIPIENT WITNESS DISCLOSURE. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED FROM JAN-21-2010 TO ASBESTOS MOTION AT FEB-04-2010, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. BASED ON THE EVIDENCE AND ARGUMENT, THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS ENGAGED IN WILLFUL DISOBEDIENCE OF THE COURT'S DISCOVERY ORDER BY ENGAGING IN A PATTERN OF DELIBERATE OVER DISCLOSURE SOMETIMES REFERRED TO AS BOXED CAR DISCOVERY. THE DISCLOSURE SHALL BE NARROWLY TAILORED & WILL CONTAIN COMPLETE INFORMATION WHICH WILL BE SUPPLEMENTED WITH A DECLARATION UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY FROM A LAWYER KNOWLEDGEABLE OF THE INVESTIGATION ON OR BEFORE 1/26/10. THE DISCOVERY ORDER PREVIOUSLY WILL BE MODIFIED THAT IT WILL REMAIN OPEN UNTIL ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL DEPT. THE DISCOVERY IS FURTHER MODIFIED & AMENDED, ALTHOUGH THE COURT DOES NOT RECALL OR BELIEVE THAT PLAINTIFF RAISED ANY OBJECTION TO THIS DISCOVERY ORDER ON THE BASIS OF THE WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE PURSUANT TO SNYDER V. CATERPILLAR, INC. 157 CAL.APP.4TH 1530, THE COURT MAKES A FINDING THAT THE RECIPROCAL DISCOVERY ORDER IS AIMED AT ACCOMPLISHING & SUSTAINING THE PARTIES RIGHTS TO DUE PROCESS IN ORDER TO PREPARE FOR A TRIAL THAT WAS SET ON 110 DAYS NOTICE. THE COURT OVERCOMES ANY ASSERTION OF THE WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE WITH RESPECT TO THE DISCLOSURE OF THE NAMES, ADDRESSES & PHONE NUMBERS OF WITNESSES & EVIDENCE BECAUSE A PARTY AND ANY DEFENDANT WOULD BE UNFAIRLY PREJUDICED IF NORMAL DISCOVERY RULES HAD TO BE FOLLOWED IN ORDER TO GET THIS CASE PREPARED FOR TRIAL. THE COURT SPECIFICALLY RESERVES THE ISSUE OF WHETHER OR NOT TRIAL NEEDS TO BE VACATED TO ACCOMMODATE THE DUE PROCESS RIGHTS OF THE MOVING PARTIES. SANCTION HEARING SET FOR 2/4/10. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: R.VELUZ (PLTF'S CSR: RITA LERNER #3179)

BASED ON THE EVIDENCE AND ARGUMENT, THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS ENGAGED IN WILLFUL DISOBEDIENCE OF THE COURT'S DISCOVERY ORDER BY ENGAGING IN A PATTERN OF DELIBERATE OVER DISCLOSURE SOMETIMES REFERRED TO AS BOXED CAR DISCOVERY. THE DISCLOSURE SHALL BE NARROWLY TAILORED & WILL CONTAIN COMPLETE INFORMATION WHICH WILL BE SUPPLEMENTED WITH A DECLARATION UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY FROM A LAWYER KNOWLEDGEABLE OF THE INVESTIGATION ON OR BEFORE 1/26/10. THE DISCOVERY ORDER PREVIOUSLY WILL BE MODIFIED THAT IT WILL REMAIN OPEN UNTIL ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL DEPT. THE DISCOVERY IS FURTHER MODIFIED & AMENDED, ALTHOUGH THE COURT DOES NOT RECALL OR BELIEVE THAT PLAINTIFF RAISED ANY OBJECTION TO THIS DISCOVERY ORDER ON THE BASIS OF THE WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE PURSUANT TO SNYDER V. CATERPILLAR, INC. 157 CAL.APP.4TH 1530, THE COURT MAKES A FINDING THAT THE RECIPROCAL DISCOVERY ORDER IS AIMED AT ACCOMPLISHING & SUSTAINING THE PARTIES RIGHTS TO DUE PROCESS IN ORDER TO PREPARE FOR A TRIAL THAT WAS SET ON 110 DAYS NOTICE. THE COURT OVERCOMES ANY ASSERTION OF THE WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE WITH RESPECT TO THE DISCLOSURE OF THE NAMES, ADDRESSES & PHONE NUMBERS OF WITNESSES & EVIDENCE BECAUSE A PARTY AND ANY DEFENDANT WOULD BE UNFAIRLY PREJUDICED IF NORMAL DISCOVERY RULES HAD TO BE FOLLOWED IN ORDER TO GET THIS CASE PREPARED FOR TRIAL. THE COURT SPECIFICALLY RESERVES THE ISSUE OF WHETHER OR NOT TRIAL NEEDS TO BE VACATED TO ACCOMMODATE THE DUE PROCESS RIGHTS OF THE MOVING PARTIES. SANCTION HEARING SET FOR 2/4/10. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: R.VELUZ (PLTF'S CSR: RITA LERNER #3179)

ASBESTOS MOTION IN DEPT. 206, DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S JOINDER IN DEFENDANT CERTAINTEED CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S COMPLIANCE REGARDING WORKSITE PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND PERCIPIENT WITNESS DISCLOSURE. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED FROM JAN-21-2010 TO ASBESTOS MOTION AT FEB-04-2010, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. BASED ON THE EVIDENCE AND ARGUMENT, THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS ENGAGED IN WILLFUL DISOBEDIENCE OF THE COURT'S DISCOVERY ORDER BY ENGAGING IN A PATTERN OF DELIBERATE OVER DISCLOSURE SOMETIMES REFERRED TO AS BOXED CAR DISCOVERY. THE DISCLOSURE SHALL BE NARROWLY TAILORED & WILL CONTAIN COMPLETE INFORMATION WHICH WILL BE SUPPLEMENTED WITH A DECLARATION UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY FROM A LAWYER KNOWLEDGEABLE OF THE INVESTIGATION ON OR BEFORE 1/26/10. THE DISCOVERY ORDER PREVIOUSLY WILL BE MODIFIED THAT IT WILL REMAIN OPEN UNTIL ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL DEPT. THE DISCOVERY IS FURTHER MODIFIED & AMENDED, ALTHOUGH THE COURT DOES NOT RECALL OR BELIEVE THAT PLAINTIFF RAISED ANY OBJECTION TO THIS DISCOVERY ORDER ON THE BASIS OF THE WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE PURSUANT TO SNYDER V. CATERPILLAR, INC. 157 CAL.APP.4TH 1530, THE COURT MAKES A FINDING THAT THE RECIPROCAL DISCOVERY ORDER IS AIMED AT ACCOMPLISHING & SUSTAINING THE PARTIES RIGHTS TO DUE PROCESS IN ORDER TO PREPARE FOR A TRIAL THAT WAS SET ON 110 DAYS NOTICE. THE COURT OVERCOMES ANY ASSERTION OF THE WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE WITH RESPECT TO THE DISCLOSURE OF THE NAMES, ADDRESSES & PHONE NUMBERS OF WITNESSES & EVIDENCE BECAUSE A PARTY AND ANY DEFENDANT WOULD BE UNFAIRLY PREJUDICED IF NORMAL DISCOVERY RULES HAD TO BE FOLLOWED IN ORDER TO GET THIS CASE PREPARED FOR TRIAL. THE COURT SPECIFICALLY RESERVES THE ISSUE OF WHETHER OR NOT TRIAL NEEDS TO BE VACATED TO ACCOMMODATE THE DUE PROCESS RIGHTS OF THE MOVING PARTIES. SANCTION HEARING SET FOR 2/4/10. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: R.VELUZ (PLTF'S CSR: RITA LERNER #3179)

ASBESTOS MOTION IN DEPT. 206, DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S JOINDER IN DEFENDANT CERTAINTEED CORPORATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S COMPLIANCE REGARDING WORKSITE PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND PERCIPIENT WITNESS DISCLOSURE. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED FROM JAN-21-2010 TO ASBESTOS MOTION AT FEB-04-2010, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206. BASED ON THE EVIDENCE AND ARGUMENT, THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS ENGAGED IN WILLFUL DISOBEDIENCE OF THE COURT'S DISCOVERY ORDER BY ENGAGING IN A PATTERN OF DELIBERATE OVER DISCLOSURE SOMETIMES REFERRED TO AS BOXED CAR DISCOVERY. THE DISCLOSURE SHALL BE NARROWLY TAILORED & WILL CONTAIN COMPLETE INFORMATION WHICH WILL BE SUPPLEMENTED WITH A DECLARATION UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY FROM A LAWYER KNOWLEDGEABLE OF THE INVESTIGATION ON OR BEFORE 1/26/10. THE DISCOVERY ORDER PREVIOUSLY WILL BE MODIFIED THAT IT WILL REMAIN OPEN UNTIL ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL DEPT. THE DISCOVERY IS FURTHER MODIFIED & AMENDED, ALTHOUGH THE COURT DOES NOT RECALL OR BELIEVE THAT PLAINTIFF RAISED ANY OBJECTION TO THIS DISCOVERY ORDER ON THE BASIS OF THE WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE PURSUANT TO SNYDER V. CATERPILLAR, INC. 157 CAL.APP.4TH 1530, THE COURT MAKES A FINDING THAT THE RECIPROCAL DISCOVERY ORDER IS AIMED AT ACCOMPLISHING & SUSTAINING THE PARTIES RIGHTS TO DUE PROCESS IN ORDER TO PREPARE FOR A TRIAL THAT WAS SET ON 110 DAYS NOTICE. THE COURT OVERCOMES ANY ASSERTION OF THE WORK PRODUCT DOCTRINE WITH RESPECT TO THE DISCLOSURE OF THE NAMES, ADDRESSES & PHONE NUMBERS OF WITNESSES & EVIDENCE BECAUSE A PARTY AND ANY DEFENDANT WOULD BE UNFAIRLY PREJUDICED IF NORMAL DISCOVERY RULES HAD TO BE FOLLOWED IN ORDER TO GET THIS CASE PREPARED FOR TRIAL. THE COURT SPECIFICALLY RESERVES THE ISSUE OF WHETHER OR NOT TRIAL NEEDS TO BE VACATED TO ACCOMMODATE THE DUE PROCESS RIGHTS OF THE MOVING PARTIES. SANCTION HEARING SET FOR 2/4/10. JUDGE: JAMES J. MCBRIDE; CLERK: R.VELUZ (PLTF'S CSR: RITA LERNER #3179)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Naomi Fribourg, Esq. of HOWARD ROME MARTIN & RIDLEY LLP (650-365-7715, X221), appearing for defendant / moving party Eaton Corporation, as SII to Cutler-Hammer, Inc.
Bruce Jackson, Esq. of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON (415-775-1206), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party JOHN AYER


RULING:
Defendant Eaton Corporation AS SII TO CUTLER-HAMMER, INC.'s motion for summary judgment. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production by presenting plaintiff's factually devoid discovery responses. Plaintiff's argument that the responses can not be used because they are unverified lacks merit. Statements contained in the special interrogatories are admissible as statements of a party or the party's authorized agent. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Brendan J. Tuohy, Esq. of K&L GATES LLP (415-882-8200), appearing for defendant / moving party Crane Co.
Stephen M. Fishback, Esq. of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON (415-775-1206), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party JOHN AYER

RULING:
Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment. Argued and tentative ruling adopted. Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Crane Co.'s alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to exposure (issues 1-4), false representation (issue 5), and intentional tort (issue 6), and granted as to punitive damages (issue 7). Mr. Throne's declaration presents a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos in original packing attributable to Crane Co. Crane Co.'s request for a continuance of the hearing in order to complete Mr. Throne's deposition is denied. Crane Co.'s objections to Mr. Throne's declaration are overruled. Crane Co. failed to shift the burden on the claims for false representation and intentional tort because it failed to propound any 'all facts' interrogatories on these claims and did not ask plaintiff any questions about these claims at his deposition. Crane Co. satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff failed to create a triable issue whether Crane Co. acted with fraud, malice, or oppression by clear and convincing evidence. Because the failure of plaintiff's counsel to cite page and line numbers in their evidence in opposition to Crane Co.'s motion contravenes CRC 3.1350(f) and imposes additional burdens on the court, plaintiffs' counsel is admonished to do so in the future. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 = (220/HEK)

RULING:
Defendant General Cable Corporation's motion for summary judgment. Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied. General Cable failed to satisfy its burden of production because it did not present with its moving papers plaintiff's verified substantive responses to contention interrogatories showing that plaintiff has no evidence and cannot reasonably obtain any evidence supporting his claims. MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Jan-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Christopher R. Nelson, Esq. of VASQUEZ ESTRADA & DUMONT LLP (415-453-1555), specially appearing for defendant / moving party FDCC CALIFORNIA INC.
No other appearances.

RULING:
Defendant FDCC California, Inc.'s motion to quash service of plaintiff's summons. No argument was made and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Defendant's motion is granted. Plaintiff's summons fails to specify that FDCC was being served as a Doe. CCP 474 provides that a summons must provide a DOE defendant with notice of the fictitious name under which it was designated in the complaint. In Armstrong v. Superior Court (1956) 144 Cal.App.2d 420, 424 the court held that when a summons fails to identify the fictitious name under which a defendant is being sued, the 'effect is to make the service of such a defective summons insufficient to give the court jurisdiction over the person of the defendant.' (See also Carol Gilbert, Inc. v. Haller 179 Cal.App.4th 852). Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
